article.title: Primary reverse total shoulder arthroplasty in patients aged ≤65 years: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
article.authors: Stamiris D, Stamiris S, Solovos EM
article.abstract: Reverse total shoulder arthroplasty (rTSA), once reserved for elderly low-demand patients, is increasingly used in younger adults despite concerns regarding complication rates and longevity. This systematic review and meta-analysis evaluate contemporary evidence on primary rTSA in patients aged ≤65 years.
